algorithms.title: Matrix Traversal Patterns
algorithms.description: Matrix traversal patterns solve problems involving 2D grids: spiral order traversal, 90-degree rotation in-place, diagonal traversal, and searching in a sorted matrix. These patterns appear frequently in interviews and rely on careful boundary tracking or mathematical index transformations.
algorithms.intuition: Spiral: maintain 4 pointers (top, bottom, left, right). Peel one layer at a time from outside in. Rotate 90° clockwise: transpose (swap matrix[i][j] with matrix[j][i]) then reverse each row. This is O(1) space, O(N²) time. Search in sorted matrix: treat as sorted array (element at [i][j] = flat[i*cols+j]) and binary search, OR use staircase search from top-right: if too big go down, if too small go left.
algorithms.steps: [{"step":1,"description":"Spiral: top=0,bottom=N-1,left=0,right=M-1. While top<=bottom && left<=right: traverse top row left→right, right col top→bottom, bottom row right→left, left col bottom→top. Shrink bounds."},{"step":2,"description":"Rotate 90° CW: Step 1: transpose (swap grid[i][j] and grid[j][i]). Step 2: reverse each row."},{"step":3,"description":"Search sorted matrix: start at top-right corner [0][cols-1]. If value == target: found. If value > target: move left. If value < target: move down."}]
algorithms.timeComplexity: Spiral: O(NM) | Rotate: O(N²) | Search: O(N+M)
algorithms.spaceComplexity: Spiral: O(NM) result | Rotate: O(1) in-place | Search: O(1)

variants.title: Diagonal traversal
variants.desc: Traverse matrix diagonals; alternate direction each diagonal

variants.title: Set matrix zeroes
variants.desc: If element is 0, zero out entire row and column

variants.title: Game of Life
variants.desc: Next state of matrix cell based on neighbors
